title: Planetary Dominoes - How Microbes Drive Biogeochemical Cycles
author: Nadia Szeinbaum
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8wrhxd6

steps.instruction: Print this file into a poster, cut each piece and add an adhesive magnet. These will be the domino pieces, and they are now able to stick to any magnetic surface. The pieces here are enough for at least three different sets, as they repeat. The design was created by Image Alleviation , in collaboration with us, specifically for this project. Planetary Domino.pdf

After a brief introduction about microbes in the environment (good vs. bad, or biogeochemical cycles, depending on the audience), ask your audience to get into groups or one by one add pieces of the domino to the board. It is key is to let them discuss and ask questions, and only if necessary explain why certain pieces might not go together, etc.
